name.en-US: Disputed Questions on the Power of God

description.en-US: Ten disputed questions, conducted at Rome — on the divine power, especially as it bears on creation, the Trinity, and the procession of the Holy Spirit.
description.la: Decem quaestiones disputatae apud Romam — de potentia divina, praesertim in creatione, Trinitate, et processione Spiritus Sancti.

sources.description: English translation by the English Dominican Fathers, *On the Power of God* (Burns Oates & Washbourne, London, 1932–34), revised by the Aquinas Institute. Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Question 1 — On the power of God
Prologue — On the power of God
Article 1 — Whether there is power in God
Article 2 — Whether God’s power is infinite
Article 3 — Whether things that are impossible for nature are possible for God
Article 4 — Whether we ought to judge of a thing’s possibility or impossibility in reference to its lower or its higher causes
Article 5 — Whether God can do what he does not do and can leave undone what he does
Article 6 — Whether God can do things that are possible to others
Article 7 — Why God is called omnipotent
Question 2 — On the generative power in God
Prologue — On the generative power in God
Article 1 — Whether within the divine there is the power to beget
Article 2 — Whether the generative power in God is spoken of essentially or notionally
Article 3 — Whether the generative power goes into the act of generation at the command of the will
Article 4 — Whether there can be several sons in God
Article 5 — Whether the generative power is included in omnipotence
Article 6 — Whether the power to beget is the same as the power to create
Question 3 — On creation, the first effect of the divine power
Prologue — On creation, the first effect of the divine power
Article 1 — Whether God can create something out of nothing
Article 2 — Whether creation is a change
Article 3 — Whether creation is something real in the creature
Article 4 — Whether the creative power is communicable to a creature
Article 5 — Whether there can be anything that is not created by God
Article 6 — Whether there is but one principle of creation
Article 7 — Whether God works in the activity of nature
Article 8 — Whether God works in nature by creating
Article 9 — Whether the rational soul is brought into being by creation or by the transmission of seed
Article 10 — Whether the rational soul is created in or apart from the body
Article 11 — Whether the sensitive and vegetative souls are created or transmitted through the seed
Article 12 — Whether the sensitive or vegetative souls are in the seed when it is separated
Article 13 — Whether any being that is from another can be eternal
Article 14 — Whether it is possible for that which differs essentially from God to have existed always
Article 15 — Whether things proceeded from God of natural necessity or by a decree of his will
Article 16 — Whether many things can proceed from one first thing
Article 17 — Whether the world has always existed
Article 18 — Whether the angels were created before the visible world
Article 19 — Whether it would be possible for the angels to exist before the visible world
Question 4 — On the creation of formless matter
Prologue — On the creation of formless matter
Article 1 — Whether the creation of formless matter preceded in duration the creation of things
Article 2 — Whether the formation of matter occurred all at once or by degrees
Question 5 — On the preservation of things in existence by God
Prologue — On the preservation of things in existence by God
Article 1 — Whether things are preserved in their being by God, or, if all God’s action were circumscribed, they would remain in being by themselves
Article 2 — Whether God can make a creature able of itself to keep itself in existence independently of God
Article 3 — Whether God can annihilate a creature
Article 4 — Whether any creature ought to be annihilated or even has been annihilated
Article 5 — Whether the movement of the heavens will cease at some time
Article 6 — Whether man can know when the movement of the heavens will cease
Article 7 — Whether the elements will remain when the heavens cease to be in motion
Article 8 — Whether action and passion will remain in the elements after the heavens have ceased to be in motion
Article 9 — Whether plants, brute animals, and minerals will remain after the end of the world
Article 10 — Whether human bodies will remain after the heavenly movement has ceased
Question 6 — On miracles
Prologue — On miracles
Article 1 — Whether God can do anything in creatures that is beyond or against nature, or contrary to the course of nature
Article 2 — Whether everything that God does without natural causes or contrary to the course of nature can be called a miracle
Article 3 — Whether spiritual creatures can work miracles by their natural power
Article 4 — Whether good angels and men can work miracles by some gift of grace
Article 5 — Whether the demons also cooperate in the working of miracles
Article 6 — Whether angels and demons have bodies naturally united to them
Article 7 — Whether angels or demons can assume bodies
Article 8 — Whether an angel or demon by means of an assumed body can exercise the functions of a living body
Article 9 — Whether the working of a miracle should be attributed to faith
Article 10 — Whether by means of certain sensible and corporeal objects, deeds, or words, the demons are forced to work the miracles that seem to occur by magic
Question 7 — On the simplicity of the divine essence
Prologue — On the simplicity of the divine essence
Article 1 — Whether God is simple
Article 2 — Whether God’s essence or substance is the same as his existence
Article 3 — Whether God is contained in a genus
Article 4 — Whether good, wise, just, and the like predicate an accident in God
Article 5 — Whether these terms signify the divine substance
Article 6 — Whether these terms are synonymous
Article 7 — Whether these terms are said of God and creatures univocally or equivocally
Article 8 — Whether there is any relation between God and the creature
Article 9 — Whether these relations between creatures and God are really in the creatures themselves
Article 10 — Whether God is really related to the creature such that this relation is something in God
Article 11 — Whether these temporal relations are in God according to account or reason
Question 8 — On those things that are said of God relatively from eternity
Prologue — On those things that are said of God relatively from eternity
Article 1 — Whether the relations said of God from eternity that are indicated by the names “Father” and “Son” are real or only relations of account or reason
Article 2 — Whether relation in God is his substance
Article 3 — Whether the relations constitute and distinguish the persons or hypostases
Article 4 — Whether, if relation is removed by the intellect, the hypostasis remains in the divine
Question 9 — On the divine persons
Prologue — On the divine persons
Article 1 — How the persons are compared with the essence, subsistence, and hypostasis
Article 2 — What a person is
Article 3 — Whether there can be a person in God
Article 4 — Whether this term “person” signifies something relative or something absolute in God
Article 5 — Whether there is a number of persons in God
Article 6 — Whether, when we speak of God, we may rightly use the word “person” in the plural
Article 7 — How numerical terms are predicated of the divine persons, whether positively or only negatively
Article 8 — Whether there is any diversity in God
Article 9 — Whether in God there are only three persons, or more or fewer than three
Question 10 — On the procession of the divine persons
Prologue — On the procession of the divine persons
Article 1 — Whether there is any procession in the divine persons
Article 2 — Whether there is only one procession in God or more than one
Article 3 — On the order between procession and relation in God
Article 4 — Whether the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Son
Article 5 — Whether the Holy Spirit would still be distinct from the Son if he did not proceed from him
